## Description

The `publish-candidate` recipe in `justfile.gh` passes `-f "create-release=..."` to `release.yml`, but the upstream workflow does not declare a `create-release` input. GitHub rejects the dispatch with HTTP 422. This was introduced in commit `ad09ebd` (#463), which added `create-release` to the downstream workflow and updated both `justfile.gh` files, but did not add the input to the upstream `.github/workflows/release.yml`.

- [ ] Breaking change (`!`) -- This change breaks backward compatibility

## Changes Made

- `justfile.gh` ‚Äî removed `create-release` parameter and `-f "create-release=..."` flag from `publish-candidate` recipe
- `assets/workspace/.devcontainer/justfile.gh` ‚Äî auto-synced by `sync-manifest` hook
- `CHANGELOG.md` / `assets/workspace/.devcontainer/CHANGELOG.md` ‚Äî added Fixed entry for #479
- `README.md`, `CONTRIBUTE.md` ‚Äî regenerated by `generate-docs` hook

## Changelog Entry

### Fixed

- **`publish-candidate` recipe sends unknown `create-release` input** ([#479](https://github.com/vig-os/devcontainer/issues/479))
  - Remove `create-release` parameter and `-f` flag from upstream `justfile.gh`; the input was added to the downstream workflow only but the recipe was updated in both places

## Testing

- [ ] Tests pass locally (`just test`)
- [x] Manual testing performed (describe below)

### Manual Testing Details

Verified that `just publish-candidate 0.3.2` no longer includes `create-release` in the `gh workflow run` arguments by inspecting the recipe source.

## Additional Notes

The downstream template (`assets/workspace/.devcontainer/justfile.gh`) has the same `create-release` parameter, but its target workflow (`assets/workspace/.github/workflows/release.yml`) does declare `create-release`, so the downstream recipe is correct and unchanged.

Refs: #479
